question: What is the NTSB report identifier for this investigation?
On what date did the Carol Jean flood and sink?
At what time (eastern daylight time) did the Carol Jean's EPIRB signal activate?
How far east of Tybee Island was the Carol Jean's last reported location at the time of the EPIRB signal?
reference_answer: About 13 miles east of Tybee Island, Georgia

question: What was the estimated property damage from the loss of the Carol Jean?
How many persons were on board when the vessel sank?
What is the case number assigned by the NTSB to this investigation?
How long was the Carol Jean, in feet?
In what year was the Carol Jean built?
What was the hull material of the Carol Jean?
What was the rated horsepower of the Carol Jean's engine?
What manufacturer made the Carol Jean's diesel engine?
Where was the Carol Jean normally docked?
What was the name of the second vessel the captain purchased and intended to tow?
From what city did the captain purchase the Having Faith?
On what date did the captain purchase the Having Faith?
How long was the rope used as the tow line, approximately?
Where on the Having Faith did the captain find the rope used as the tow line?
reference_answer: In a forward compartment of the Having Faith

question: According to the captain's stated reasoning, how long had the tow line been in use before he believed it had chafed and parted?
At what time did the Carol Jean depart with the Having Faith in tow?
At what time did the tow line part?
After the tow line fouled the propeller, in approximately how many feet of water did the captain drop the Carol Jean's anchor?
At what time did the captain's daughter contact Coast Guard Sector Charleston?
At what time did the Coast Guard small boat arrive on scene the night of March 16?
Between the original anchoring and 0713 on March 17, in what direction did the Carol Jean drag?
reference_answer: Northwest — by 0713 the Carol Jean was about 7.5 miles northwest of where it had originally anchored

question: At what time on March 17 did the Coast Guard helicopter arrive to rescue the captain and his friend?
From which city did the rescue helicopter launch?
How did the captain and his friend leave the Carol Jean during the helicopter rescue?
reference_answer: They donned lifejackets, jumped into the water, and swam away from the vessel to avoid the helicopter's rescue basket tangling in the vessel's gear

question: On what date did the Having Faith break apart on the shoreline?
reference_answer: March 19, 2023 (the day after the Having Faith was located aground on March 18)

question: At which jetty location was the Having Faith found aground?
reference_answer: A jetty at St. Phillips Island, Georgia

question: What component on the Carol Jean did the captain discover had been damaged after the diver removed the tow line on March 19?
What did the captain take to a local repair shop on March 19?
According to the report, what was the probable cause of the sinking?
reference_answer: Likely flooding from an unknown source while the vessel was anchored offshore and unattended

question: According to the report, what contributing factor was identified?
reference_answer: The captain's inadequate planning for a tow, leading to the Carol Jean being anchored after the tow line failed and fouled its propeller

question: When was the Carol Jean's last Coast Guard dockside safety examination conducted?
How many deficiencies did the Coast Guard issue for the Carol Jean at that examination?
According to the report, what three deficiencies did the captain still plan to correct after towing the Having Faith back?
reference_answer: Obtaining a Federal Communications Commission license; installing guards on the generator's exposed belts; and repainting the homeport name

question: Under what statute are commercial fishing vessel dockside safety examinations required, according to the report?
reference_answer: The Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2010

question: How frequently are dockside safety examinations required under that authority for the kind of operation the Carol Jean performed?
reference_answer: Once every 5 years (for fishing vessels that operate 3 nautical miles beyond the baseline)

question: According to the report, did the captain or his friend hold a Coast Guard-issued credential?
reference_answer: No; neither held a Coast Guard-issued credential, and neither was required to do so
